volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Epidicus (Section 4.1.556b - Section 5.1.614c)

sections.label: Section 4.1.556b
sections.translation: What , are you he who for your own gratification brought heavy troubles upon me ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.558
sections.translation: I am he . Health to you .

sections.label: Section 4.1.558b
sections.original: Salva sum , quia te esse salvom sentio .
sections.translation: I am in health , since I see you in health .

sections.label: Section 4.1.559
sections.translation: Give me your hand .

sections.label: Section 4.1.559b
sections.translation: Take it — you hold by the hand a woman distrest and full of woes .

sections.label: Section 4.1.560b
sections.original: Quid est quod voltus turbatust tuos ?
sections.translation: What is it that disturbs your features ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.561
sections.translation: The daughter whom I had by you —

sections.label: Section 4.1.561b
sections.translation: What of her ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.561c
sections.translation: When I had brought her up , I lost her ; she fell into the hands of the enemy .

sections.label: Section 4.1.562b
sections.original: Habe animum lenem et tranquillum . tace .
sections.translation: Keep your mind in quiet and at rest . Why look , she ’ s here at my house safe and sound . For immediately I heard from my servant that she was a captive , instantly I gave the money for her to be purchased ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.565
sections.original: ut emeretur . ille eam rem ádeo sobrie et frugaliter
sections.translation: he managed this affair as discreetly and frugally as in other matters he is egregiously — dishonest .

sections.label: Section 4.1.567
sections.original: Fac videam , si mea , si salva mihi sit .
sections.translation: Let me see her , whether it is she or no .

sections.label: Section 4.1.567b
sections.translation: Hallo there ! you — Canthara , this instant bid my daughter [*] come out before the house , that she may see her mother .

sections.label: Section 4.1.569b
sections.translation: My spirits now at last return to me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.570
sections.original: Quid est , pater , quod me excivisti ante aedis ?
sections.translation: Why is it , father , that you have called me out before the house ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.570b
sections.translation: That you may see and accost your mother , and wish her health on her arrival , and give her kisses .

sections.label: Section 4.2.572
sections.translation: What mother of mine ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.572b
sections.translation: She who , half dead , is following your gaze .

sections.label: Section 4.2.573
sections.original: Quis istaec est quam tu osculum mihi ferre iubes ?
sections.translation: Who is this that you are requesting to kiss me ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.573b
sections.translation: Your own daughter .

sections.label: Section 4.2.574
sections.translation: What , she ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.574b
sections.translation: She .

sections.label: Section 4.2.574c
sections.translation: What — am I to kiss her ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.574d
sections.original: Quor non , quae ex te nata sit ?
sections.translation: Why not , her who was born of you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.575
sections.translation: Man , you are mad .

sections.label: Section 4.2.575b
sections.translation: What , I ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.575c
sections.translation: Yes , you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.575d
sections.translation: Why ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.575e
sections.translation: Because this woman — I neither know nor understand who she is , nor have I beheld her with my eyes before this day .

sections.label: Section 4.2.577
sections.original: Scio quid erres : quia vestitum atque ornatum immutabilem
sections.translation: I know why you are mistaken ; because this woman has her dress and ornaments changed .

sections.label: Section 4.2.579
sections.original: ⋯ aliter catuli longe olent , aliter suis .
sections.translation: Puppies have one smell [*] , pigs quite another ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.580
sections.translation: I say that I do not know her , who she is .

sections.label: Section 4.2.580b
sections.translation: Oh ! by our trust in Gods and men , what is this ? Am I following the calling of a Procurer , to be keeping strange women in my house , and to be emptying my house of my money ? ( To ACROPOLISTIS .) What are you to be calling me your father and kissing me ? Why stand you stupidly there ? Why do you keep silent ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.584
sections.translation: What do you want me to say ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.584b
sections.original: Haec negat se tuam esse matrem .
sections.translation: She denies that she is your mother .

sections.label: Section 4.2.584c
sections.translation: Don ’ t let her be so ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.585
sections.original: si non volt : equidem hac invita támen ero matris filia ;
sections.translation: if she don ’ t choose . For my own part , whether she likes it or not , I shall be my mother ’ s daughter still . It isn ’ t right for me to compel this woman to be my mother if she doesn ’ t like .

sections.label: Section 4.2.587
sections.translation: Why then did you call me father ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.587b
sections.translation: That is your own fault , not mine ; ought I not to call you father when you call me daughter ? Her too , as well ( pointing to PHILIPPA ) if she were to call me daughter , I should call mother .

sections.label: Section 4.2.590
sections.original: negat haec filiám me suam ésse : non ergo haec mater mea est .
sections.translation: She declares that I am not her daughter ; then she is not my mother . In fine , this is no fault of mine ; what I ’ ve been taught , I ’ ve told you all of it . Epidicus was my instructor .

sections.label: Section 4.2.592b
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ! I ’ ve upset my waggon [*] !

sections.label: Section 4.2.593
sections.translation: Have I done anything amiss towards that ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.593b
sections.translation: Upon my faith , if I ever hear you call me father , I ’ ll put an end to your life , you jade !

sections.label: Section 4.2.594b
sections.translation: I shan ’ t call you so .

sections.label: Section 4.2.595
sections.original: ubi voles pater esse , ibi esto ; ubi noles , ne fueris pater .
sections.translation: When you want to be my father , then be so ; when you don ’ t want , don ’ t be my father .

sections.label: Section 4.2.596
sections.original: Quid , si ob eam rem hanc emisti , quia tuam gnatam es ratus ,
sections.translation: What ? Did you purchase her for that reason , because you supposed her to be your daughter ? By what signs did you recognize her ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.597b
sections.translation: By none .

sections.label: Section 4.2.597c
sections.translation: Why did you suppose her to be our daughter ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.598b
sections.translation: My servant Epidicus told me so .

sections.label: Section 4.2.599
sections.original: Quid si servo aliter visumst , non poteras novisse , obsecro ? †
sections.translation: What if it had seemed to your servant otherwise ? Prithee , could you not have known ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.600
sections.original: Quid ego , qui illam ut primum vidi , numquam vidi postea ?
sections.translation: How should I , who had never seen her after having once beheld her .

sections.label: Section 4.2.601
sections.translation: Wretched creature , I ’ m quite undone !

sections.label: Section 4.2.601b
sections.original: Ne fle , mulier . intro abi , habe animum bonum ;
sections.translation: Don ’ t weep , madam ; go in-doors ; be of good courage ; I ’ ll find her out .

sections.label: Section 4.2.602b
sections.original: Hinc Athenis civis eam émit Atticus :
sections.translation: An Attic citizen from Athens here purchased her Indeed , they said it was a young man who had bought her .

sections.label: Section 4.2.603b
sections.translation: I will find her ; hold your peace . Only do go in-doors , and keep an eye upon this Circe [*] , this daughter of the Sun .

sections.label: Section 4.2.605
sections.original: ego relictis rebus Epidicum operam quaerendo dabo :
sections.translation: All business laid aside , I ’ ll give my attention to seeking for Epidicus . If I find him , I ’ ll make this day become the final one for him .

sections.label: Section 5.1.607
sections.original: Male morigerus mi est danista , qui a me argentum non petit
sections.translation: The Banker [*] is inattentive to me , not to seek the money of me , or bring this woman who has been purchased out of the spoil . But see — here comes Epidicus How ’ s this , that in gloominess his brow is wrinkled ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.610
sections.original: Si undecim deos praeter sese secum adducat Iuppiter ,
sections.translation: If Jupiter unto himself were to take the eleven Gods [*] beside himself , even then , all of them would not be able to rescue Epidicus from torture . I ’ ve seen Periphanes buying the thongs ; Apaecides was together with him ; now , I do believe that these persons are in search of me . They have found it out ; they know that they ’ ve been imposed upon .

sections.label: Section 5.1.614b
sections.translation: What are you about , my ready occasion ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.614c
sections.translation: That which a wretched fellow is about .
